text: Document .bowman data layout and git interaction clearly
description: Explain what Aimparency writes into `.bowman`, which files are generated vs user-authored, what belongs in `.gitignore`, and how this fits normal git workflows. This should be understandable from docs and reinforced in the UI where relevant.

reflections.context: Document Aimparency's .bowman data layout and git interaction clearly for first-time open source users.
reflections.outcome: Added a dedicated Git Workflow section to the root README that explains which .bowman files are normally committed, which are generated cache/search artifacts, and that agent-instruction injection can also modify repo-root config files.
reflections.effectiveness: Checking the backend implementation before writing docs exposed the important exception around injected root-level agent files, which made the final guidance more accurate.
reflections.lesson: For local-first tools, the git story should be written from the filesystem behavior upward rather than from the idealized architecture.
reflections.pattern: This follows the same pattern as earlier release-doc work: read the runtime first, then document the exact user-visible consequences.

status.comment: Expanded the root README with an explicit git workflow section covering what .bowman files are user-authored vs generated, what should stay ignored, and how multiple local projects stay separate.
